diagrams.title: Addressing Modes Classification
diagrams.description: Classification tree showing different types of addressing modes used in computer architecture
elements: Memory Addressing (Direct, Indirect, Indexed, Relative, Base-Register, Stack)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the hierarchy and categorization of addressing modes

diagrams.title: RISC vs CISC Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of RISC and CISC architectures across multiple dimensions
elements: RISC: Simple ops, Fixed length, Load-store, Hardwired, Few addressing modes
CISC: Complex ops, Variable length, Memory-to-memory, Microcoded, Many addressing modes
Performance metrics, Power consumption, Code density comparison
diagrams.purpose: Visual comparison to understand architectural differences and trade-offs

diagrams.title: Indexed Addressing Example
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how indexed addressing works for array element access
elements: Base Address (1000), Index Register (R3=5), Element Size (4 bytes)
Shows memory array with elements at consecutive addresses
Demonstrates how each index value accesses different array element
diagrams.purpose: Understanding indexed addressing for array processing

diagrams.title: RISC Pipeline Stages
diagrams.description: Five-stage pipeline diagram showing instruction execution overlap in RISC processors
elements: IF (Instruction Fetch), ID (Instruction Decode), EX (Execute), MEM (Memory Access), WB (Write Back)
Shows how 5 instructions execute simultaneously in different stages
Demonstrates pipelining efficiency in RISC architecture
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how RISC achieves high performance through pipelining
